stories.title: SGA Drops 37, But Jalen Williams Exits With Hamstring — Thunder 120, Suns 107

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
SGA got the Clutch Player of the Year trophy before tip-off — then dropped 37 pts and 9 ast.

Thunder 120, Suns 107. OKC up 2-0. Dillon Brooks 30 pts wasn't enough.

#ThunderUp

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Thunder win Game 2 120-107, but Jalen Williams left in Q3 with a left hamstring injury.

He already missed 30 games this season. OKC is up 2-0 — but at what cost? MRI pending.

#ThunderUp

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander didn't just accept the NBA Clutch Player of the Year award Wednesday night — he performed his acceptance speech on the hardwood.

SGA dropped 37 points on 13-of-25 shooting with 9 assists as the Oklahoma City Thunder dominated the Phoenix Suns 120-107 in Game 2, taking a commanding 2-0 first-round series lead. Chet Holmgren added 19 points in what looked like another easy evening for the defending champions.

But the night ended on an anxious note. Jalen Williams, who had 19 points through three quarters, left the game with a left hamstring injury in the third and never returned. Thunder head coach Mark Daigneault said the team believes it was "aggravated" — significant, since Williams already missed 30 games this season with a right hamstring strain.

For Phoenix, Dillon Brooks fought hard with 30 points before fouling out in the fourth quarter. Devin Booker added 22 and Jalen Green contributed 21, but the Suns couldn't overcome SGA's brilliance.

The Thunder face a real question heading into Game 3 on Sunday: Can they keep rolling without their second-best player?

#ThunderUp | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: SGA Drops 37 as Thunder Top Suns in Game 2, But Jalen Williams Exits With Hamstring Injury

stories.title: Cade 27/11 Leads Pistons Rout — Detroit Evens Series, Ends 18-Year Home Playoff Drought

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Game 1: Cade Cunningham 39 pts — Pistons lose.
Game 2: Cade 27 pts, 11 ast — Pistons dominate 98-83.

Third quarter: Detroit 38, Orlando 16. That's a statement.

Series tied 1-1. #DetroitBasketball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Detroit Pistons just ended an 11-game home playoff losing streak that started in 2008.

Cade dropped 27/11. The third quarter was 38-16. Detroit is BACK.

#DetroitBasketball

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

Cade Cunningham showed Detroit exactly why he's their franchise cornerstone — and then some.

After pouring in 39 points in Game 1 of their first-round series against Orlando and watching it result in a loss, Cunningham flipped the script entirely in Game 2. He finished with 27 points, 11 assists and 6 rebounds, but the most important stat was the third-quarter score: Detroit 38, Orlando 16.

That quarter was Cade at his most complete — creating for others, attacking the rim, making the Pistons impossible to guard. When it was over, the Pistons had an 98-83 victory and a series tied at 1-1.

But perhaps the most meaningful number is 11. As in, 11 consecutive home playoff losses for the Detroit Pistons before Wednesday night. A streak that started in 2008 and became part of the franchise's identity — over. Done. Erased.

Paolo Banchero had 18 points for Orlando and Jalen Suggs added 19, but neither could match the collective energy Detroit brought in front of a delirious home crowd.

The series heads to Orlando tied up. Advantage: anyone's guess.

#DetroitBasketball | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Cade's 27/11 and a 38-16 Third Quarter: Pistons Even Series, End 18-Year Home Playoff Curse

stories.title: Edwards 30/10 Powers Wolves Comeback — Minnesota Erases 19 Down to Stun Nuggets

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Down 19 points. Anthony Edwards responds: 30 pts, 10 reb.

Timberwolves 119, Nuggets 114. Series tied 1-1. Ant refuses to be swept in Denver.

#RaisedByWolves

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Rudy Gobert held Nikola Jokić to 8-of-20 FG. Jokić and Murray shot 2-of-12 in the fourth.

Minnesota won 119-114 after trailing by 19. Defense wins in the playoffs.

#RaisedByWolves

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Nineteen points down. Most teams fold. Anthony Edwards is not most players.

On Monday in Denver, the Minnesota Timberwolves found themselves trailing the Nuggets 44-25 in the second quarter — on the road, in a building where Denver rarely loses in the postseason. It looked like the Wolves might get blown out and fall into a 0-2 series hole.

Then Edwards decided enough was enough.

By the time the final buzzer sounded, Edwards had 30 points (10-for-25, 7-for-9 from the line) and 10 rebounds. Donte DiVincenzo hit a massive three-pointer with 1:05 left to seal it. Minnesota won 119-114 and tied the series at 1-1.

The other MVP of the night: Rudy Gobert. The big man physically challenged Nikola Jokić all night, holding the three-time MVP to 24 points on 8-of-20 shooting. Jokić and Jamal Murray combined to shoot just 2-for-12 in the fourth quarter, when it mattered most.

Murray finished with 30 points for Denver, but it wasn't enough to overcome Minnesota's collective will.

Game 3 is tonight in Denver. The Wolves are alive — and SGA's defending-champion squad just lost Jalen Williams. Nothing about these playoffs is set in stone.

#Wolves #RaisedByWolves | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Down 19, Anthony Edwards Would Not Let Minnesota Lose — Wolves Stun Nuggets 119-114 in Game 2

stories.title: Game 3 Night — Knicks-Hawks and Wolves-Nuggets Headline Thursday

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tonight's NBA playoff action:

🏀 Knicks at Hawks — 7 PM ET, ESPN
🏀 Cavs at Raptors — 8 PM ET, Prime
🏀 Wolves at Nuggets — 9:30 PM ET, ESPN

Three series. Three Game 3s. Who steps up?

#NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 4:00 PM ET

Thursday night is a Game 3 tripleheader — and every single matchup matters.

First up, the New York Knicks head to Atlanta to face the Hawks at 7 PM ET on ESPN. The series is tied 1-1 after CJ McCollum's incredible 32-point fourth-quarter surge helped Atlanta steal Game 2 at Madison Square Garden. Now the Hawks get home court for the first time — can they build on that energy?

Then Cleveland heads north to Toronto at 8 PM ET on Prime Video. The Cavaliers lead this series 2-0 and have won eight straight against the Raptors. Toronto is fighting for their postseason lives.

Finally, the marquee matchup: Minnesota visits Denver at 9:30 PM ET on ESPN for Game 3 of their tight Wolves-Nuggets series. After Minnesota's stunning 19-point comeback in Game 2, this could be one of the best games of the entire first round.

Three playoff games. Six teams. All the pressure in the world. Who wants it more?

#NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: NBA Playoffs Game 3 Thursday: Knicks-Hawks, Wolves-Nuggets and Cavs-Raptors All on the Line

stories.title: Round 1 Standings Snapshot — Three Series Tied, Thunder and Cavs Dominant

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
2026 NBA Playoffs R1 update:

⚡ Thunder 2-0 | Lakers 2-0 | Cavs 2-0
🔄 Pistons-Magic 1-1 | Wolves-Nuggets 1-1
🔄 Hawks-Knicks 1-1 | Celtics-76ers 1-1

Three sweeps brewing. Five series wide open. #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 7:00 PM ET

Here's where the 2026 NBA Playoffs first round stands after two games of every series:

Three teams are in commanding control — the Oklahoma City Thunder lead the Suns 2-0, the Los Angeles Lakers lead the Rockets 2-0 and the Cleveland Cavaliers lead the Raptors 2-0. All three favorites look sharp.

But five series are deadlocked at 1-1: the Magic and Pistons are tied, the Wolves and Nuggets are tied, the Hawks and Knicks are tied, the Celtics and 76ers are tied, and the Spurs and Blazers are tied.

That's not just parity. That's chaos. That's why we watch.

Coming up: Celtics-76ers Game 3 on Friday in Philadelphia (7 PM ET), Spurs-Blazers Game 3 Friday at Portland (10:30 PM ET), and Lakers-Rockets Game 3 Saturday in Houston.

The 2026 playoffs are delivering. Big time.

#NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: 2026 NBA Playoffs Round 1: Three Teams on the Brink of a Sweep, Five Series Perfectly Tied
